seoTitle: Export Multiple Confluence Pages at Once (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Learn how to export multiple Confluence pages in bulk using the 2026 Cloud export wizard, choose PDF or HTML, and download a complete archive safely.
intro: Opening and saving articles one at a time isn't practical when you need to archive a whole section of documentation. This guide shows how to use Confluence's bulk export tools to pull multiple pages at once into a single downloadable package.

steps.heading: Open Space Settings
steps.body: Open your target space, scroll to the bottom of the left sidebar, and click Space Settings.

steps.heading: Find the export option
steps.body: Navigate to the Manage Space Data section and select Export Content.

steps.heading: Choose a format
steps.body: Select either PDF documents or the full HTML framework, depending on how you plan to use the archive.

steps.heading: Select specific pages
steps.body: Click Custom Export to open the interactive page tree, then check only the parent and nested pages you want to include.

steps.heading: Export and download
steps.body: Click Export, wait for the cloud engine to compile your data, then click the download link to fetch the finished ZIP archive.

faq.question: Can I export just a few pages instead of the whole space?
faq.answer: Yes, choosing Custom Export lets you check specific groups of parent and nested pages instead of exporting everything.

faq.question: What format should I use to export multiple pages?
faq.answer: Choose PDF for presentation-ready documents or the full HTML framework if you need an offline, browsable copy.

faq.question: Will my images and attachments be included in the export?
faq.answer: Toggling the advanced multimedia options during export helps ensure image maps and project files stay attached to the output.

faq.question: How do I know when a large export is finished?
faq.answer: You can track completion meters in the background while the archive compiles before downloading the final ZIP file.
